results.description: A high-clarity user providing detailed, structured data across all logic model sections.
failures: turn 4: request failed: HTTP 500: <!DOCTYPE html><html><head><meta charSet="utf-8" data-next-head=""/><meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width" data-next-head=""/><style data-next-hide-fouc="true">body{display:none}</style><noscript data-next-hide-fouc="true"><style>body{display:block}</style></noscript><noscript data-n-css

turnResults.user: We are starting the Green Haven Community Garden in West Philadelphia. Our goal is to serve local residents by reducing food insecurity and providing a green space for community building.
turnResults.reply: Thanks for sharing that. What resources, materials, and expertise support this work, in addition to your current staff and funding?

turnResults.user: We have two part-time coordinators, $5,000 in seed grants, donated tools from the city, and a partnership with a local horticulture expert for training.
turnResults.reply: That's a fantastic set of resources to start with. Great, I captured those resources. What materials and expertise are still part of the work before we move to activities?

turnResults.user: We hold weekly planting workshops, maintain 20 raised beds, and run a bi-weekly harvest distribution stand.
turnResults.reply: Those sound like the core activities of the program – the workshops, maintaining the beds, and distributing the harvest. Thanks, I captured those activities. How do you ensure quality and fidelity?

results.description: A user providing partial/vague info requiring agent follow-ups and coaching.

turnResults.user: We work with high schoolers in the city to help them get jobs.
turnResults.reply: Thanks for sharing that. Thanks, I captured the impact details so far. What specific long-term goal should we capture so the impact statement is complete?

turnResults.user: Our long-term goal is 100% college or trade school enrollment for our seniors.
turnResults.reply: Excellent, that's a powerful and specific goal. Great, I captured your intended impact. Next, what resources support this work?

turnResults.user: We have mentors and some funding from a local bank.
turnResults.reply: That's great. Great, I captured those resources. What materials and expertise are still part of the work before we move to activities?

turnResults.user: Oh, we also use a local library for meeting space and a licensed curriculum.
turnResults.reply: Got it. Great, I captured those resources. What materials and expertise are still part of the work before we move to activities?
Got it, so "mentorship meetings" are a key activity. What activities should we capture next? Please describe the key actions and outputs.

turnResults.user: Each student meets with a mentor for 2 hours a week to work on college apps and career goals.
turnResults.reply: Thank you for that detail. Thanks, I captured those activities. How do you ensure quality and fidelity?

turnResults.user: We check in with the mentors often.
turnResults.reply: That's a great practice. How do you ensure quality and fidelity standards in delivery?

turnResults.user: We use a standardized mentor handbook (fidelity) and require all mentors to have background checks and 10 hours of training (quality).
turnResults.reply: That's fantastic. Great, I captured your quality and fidelity standards. What outcomes should we expect next?
Of course, their success is the ultimate goal, and that's what drives everything you do. What should short term, medium term, and long term outcomes look like?

turnResults.user: Short term is completing one college app. Medium term is getting an acceptance letter. Long term is that enrollment goal I mentioned.
turnResults.reply: Those are fantastic milestones. Great, this is complete. I captured the outcomes and can summarize the full logic model now.

results.description: All-in-one impact statement is captured and confirmed.

turnResults.user: We serve middle school students in North Philadelphia and our long-term goal is that they read on grade level and transition successfully to high school.
turnResults.reply: Thanks for sharing that. What resources, materials, and expertise support this work, in addition to your current staff and funding?
Great, I'm glad that summary feels right. What resources, materials, and expertise support this work, in addition to your current staff and funding?

results.description: When user provides a resource list, the patch captures multiple resource buckets.

turnResults.user: We have program staff, volunteers, partner counselors, curriculum materials, laptops, grant funding, and staff training.
turnResults.reply: That's a great, clear list of the resources you have in place. Great, I captured those resources. What materials and expertise are still part of the work before we move to activities?

results.description: Resource response should not be ignored across turns and should keep resources in model state.

turnResults.user: Staff mentors, school partners, donated laptops, grant funding, and evidence-based training.
turnResults.reply: Thanks, that's a very clear list of the resources you rely on. Great, I captured those resources. What materials and expertise are still part of the work before we move to activities?

turnResults.user: Anything else you need from me on resources?
turnResults.reply: That's a fair question. Great, I captured those resources. What materials and expertise are still part of the work before we move to activities?
